title: Implement agent output capture and log storage
description: Capture Goose stdout/stderr to per-task log files while streaming to terminal in real-time (FR-007).
design: In pkg/cli/agent/goose.go Execute method, use io.MultiWriter to tee output to both os.Stdout/Stderr and a log file at .agent-runs/<runID>/task-<taskID>-output.log. Create the log file before launching the process. If the file cannot be created, fall back to stdout-only with a warning. Store the relative log file path in TaskResult.OutputLog field. The RunStore should provide the artifact directory path via EnsureRunDir().
acceptance_criteria: Task output appears in terminal in real-time. Log file is created at correct path. Log content matches terminal output. TaskResult.OutputLog contains relative path to log file.

title: Register sl agent command group in Cobra
description: Add the 'sl agent' parent command and wire it into the root command. This is the entry point for run/status/logs/stop subcommands.
design: Create pkg/cli/commands/agent.go with a VarAgentCmd cobra.Command (Use 'agent', Short 'Manage AI agent task execution'). Register it in cmd/sl/main.go alongside existing commands (VarAuthCmd, VarIssueCmd, etc.). No subcommands yet — just the parent group.
acceptance_criteria: 'sl agent' displays help text. 'sl agent --help' shows the command group. Existing commands unaffected. go build succeeds.

title: User Story 2: Task Execution Monitoring and Status
description: Visibility: sl agent status shows dashboard of run progress, sl agent logs shows per-task output. Priority P2.

title: Add Goose to DefaultAgents in launcher
description: Extend the launcher package to include Goose as a recognized agent option alongside Claude Code.
design: In pkg/cli/launcher/launcher.go, add a new AgentOption to DefaultAgents: Name='Goose', Command='goose', Description='AI agent for automated task execution'. Also add InstallInstructions case for 'goose' returning 'brew install block-goose-cli'.
acceptance_criteria: DefaultAgents contains 3 entries (Claude Code, Goose, None). InstallInstructions('goose') returns brew install instruction. go build succeeds.

title: Implement run completion summary output
description: Display a well-formatted summary at the end of agent run with task counts, timing, and next steps.
design: In pkg/cli/agent/runner.go, at end of Run(): print summary block matching contracts/cli-interface.md format. Include: run ID, total duration, completed/failed/skipped counts, per-task one-liner (checkmark/X + ID + title + duration), newly unblocked tasks (if any tasks completed). If all tasks done: 'All tasks completed. Branch ready for review.' If partial: 'N tasks failed. Run sl agent logs <id> to investigate.' Use lipgloss or basic ANSI for formatting consistency with other sl commands.
acceptance_criteria: Summary displays at end of every run. Counts are accurate. Duration formatting is human-readable. Failed task IDs highlighted for easy investigation.

title: Implement spec context auto-detection from git branch
description: Auto-detect which spec to target by parsing the current git branch name, with --spec flag override (FR-014).
design: In pkg/cli/agent/context.go (or a new util file), implement DetectSpecContext(specFlag string) (string, error). If specFlag is non-empty, use it directly. Otherwise, get current branch via go-git or 'git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD', then check if specledger/<branch>/ directory exists. If so, return branch as spec context. If not, return error 'cannot auto-detect spec from branch <name>'. Also implement ValidateSpecDir(specContext string) bool that checks specledger/<specContext>/issues.jsonl exists.
acceptance_criteria: Auto-detection works for branch '599-agent-task-execution' when specledger/599-agent-task-execution/ exists. --spec flag overrides auto-detection. Clear error when spec directory doesn't exist.

title: Polish error messages and user guidance
description: Ensure all error conditions produce clear, actionable messages with next steps.
design: Review all error paths in pkg/cli/agent/ and pkg/cli/commands/agent.go. Each error should follow the pattern: 'Error: <what happened>. <suggestion>'. Key error messages: 'Goose not installed. Install with: brew install block-goose-cli', 'Git working tree has uncommitted changes. Commit or stash before running agent.', 'No tasks available for execution (all completed or blocked)', 'Cannot detect spec from branch <name>. Use --spec flag to specify.', 'Agent already running for spec <name>. Use sl agent stop first.'. Use existing pkg/cli/ui/ color utilities for consistent formatting.
acceptance_criteria: All error paths produce helpful messages. Error messages include resolution steps. Consistent formatting across all agent commands.

title: AI Agent Task Execution Service
description: Create a service to execute spec tasks for SpecLedger users via AI agents (Goose). The system reads tasks from issues.jsonl, selects ready (unblocked) tasks in priority order, constructs execution context with task metadata and design notes, launches Goose as a subprocess, captures results, and updates task status. Builds on 597-agent-model-config and 597-issue-create-fields.

title: Implement sl agent status command
description: Show dashboard view of current or most recent agent run with task progress, timing, and failure details (FR-007).
design: In pkg/cli/commands/agent.go, add VarAgentStatusCmd. Flags: --spec, --run (default latest), --json. RunE: (1) Detect spec context, (2) Create RunStore, (3) If --run specified load that run, else LoadLatest(), (4) If no runs found, print 'No agent runs found' and exit. (5) Format output as per contracts/cli-interface.md: run ID, status, started/ended timestamps, duration, per-task table (checkmark/X, task ID, title, status, duration, error details), summary counts. If --json, output AgentRun as JSON. If run.Status=='running', mark it as active. Register as subcommand of VarAgentCmd.
acceptance_criteria: sl agent status displays formatted run summary. --json outputs valid JSON. No-run case handled gracefully. Completed/failed/skipped tasks shown with correct symbols. Duration calculated correctly.

title: Implement config-driven model and provider selection
description: Use the resolved agent config hierarchy (from 597-agent-model-config) to determine Goose model and provider at runtime.
design: In pkg/cli/agent/runner.go NewRunner(): call config.ResolveAgentEnv() to get resolved env vars map. Store model and provider values from the resolved config in the AgentRun metadata fields (agent_model, agent_provider). Pass the resolved env map to GooseAdapter.BuildEnv(). In the run header output, display which model/provider is being used. If agent.model is not set, log a warning suggesting 'sl config set agent.model <model>' but allow Goose to use its own defaults.
acceptance_criteria: sl agent run header shows model and provider being used. Config values from all layers (global, profile, team, personal) correctly resolved. Missing model config produces warning but doesn't block execution. Custom profiles work (sl config profile use <name> before sl agent run).

title: Foundational Phase
description: Core infrastructure that must complete before any user story: agent types, run store, Goose adapter, task selector, execution context builder, and spec auto-detection.

title: Implement sl agent run Cobra command
description: Wire the Runner into a Cobra command with --spec, --task, --headless, --max-turns, --dry-run flags (FR-009, FR-011, FR-014).
design: In pkg/cli/commands/agent.go, add VarAgentRunCmd cobra.Command. Use 'run', Short 'Execute tasks using a Goose AI agent'. Flags: --spec string (override auto-detect), --task string (specific task ID), --headless bool (no interactive prompts), --max-turns int (default 50), --dry-run bool (preview only). RunE function: (1) Call DetectSpecContext(specFlag), (2) Create Runner, (3) Call PreflightChecks(), (4) If dry-run: print discovered tasks and exit, (5) Call runner.Run(taskFlag), (6) Print summary, (7) Return appropriate exit code (0=all success, 1=partial, 2=no tasks, 3=prereq fail). Register as subcommand of VarAgentCmd. Wire auth check via existing LoadCredentials() if needed (FR-001).
acceptance_criteria: sl agent run executes tasks and displays progress. --spec overrides auto-detection. --task runs single task. --dry-run shows tasks without executing. --headless suppresses prompts. Exit codes match contract (0/1/2/3). Auth is verified before execution.

title: User Story 3: Cloud Agent Execution
description: Headless execution: --headless flag, GOOSE_MODE=auto, auto token refresh, concurrent task claim locking. Priority P3.

title: Implement execution context builder
description: Build the instruction file that Goose receives for each task, containing task metadata, acceptance criteria, design notes, and repository context (FR-004).
design: In pkg/cli/agent/context.go, implement ContextBuilder struct with fields: specDir, repoRoot, branch string. Methods: NewContextBuilder(specDir, repoRoot, branch string). Build(task *issues.Issue, runID string) generates markdown instruction file content using the template from research.md section 4 (Task title, ID, spec context, description, acceptance criteria, DoD checklist, design notes, additional notes, repository context, instructions). WriteInstructionFile(content string, runDir string, taskID string) writes to runDir/task-<taskID>-instructions.md and returns the file path. Load optional design docs (plan.md, data-model.md) from specDir if they exist and include summary in context.
acceptance_criteria: Build() produces well-structured markdown with all task fields populated. Missing optional fields (design, notes) are gracefully omitted. Instruction file is written to correct path. go build succeeds.

title: User Story 4: Execution Configuration and Recipes
description: Customization: agent-recipe.yaml loading, config-driven model/provider, hooks. Priority P4.

title: Implement auth token auto-refresh for long runs
description: Automatically refresh Supabase auth token during extended agent runs to prevent mid-execution auth failures.
design: In pkg/cli/agent/runner.go, before each task execution, check if credentials are near expiry using auth.LoadCredentials().IsExpired() (which has a 30s buffer). If expired and refresh token available, call auth.RefreshAccessToken(). If refresh fails and headless, log error and continue (Goose may not need auth). If not headless, warn user. This uses existing pkg/cli/auth/client.go RefreshAccessToken(). For truly long runs, consider a goroutine that refreshes proactively every 30 minutes.
acceptance_criteria: Token refresh occurs automatically before expired token is used. Refresh failure in headless mode logs warning and continues. Refresh failure in interactive mode warns user. Existing auth module used — no new auth logic.

title: Implement git state verification and commit tracking
description: Verify clean git state before execution and track commits made by the agent per task (FR-015).
design: In pkg/cli/agent/runner.go PreflightChecks method, run 'git status --porcelain' and fail if output is non-empty (dirty state). In the task execution loop, before each task capture HEAD via 'git rev-parse HEAD', after task completion capture HEAD again. If they differ, collect all new commit hashes with 'git log --format=%H <old-head>..HEAD' and store in TaskResult.Commits. Verify the branch hasn't changed with 'git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD'. Use os/exec for git commands (not go-git) for simplicity and consistency with how Goose also uses git.
acceptance_criteria: PreflightChecks fails with clear error on dirty git state. Commits made by agent are captured in TaskResult.Commits. Branch name verified unchanged after each task. If agent creates unexpected branches, error is reported.

title: Implement Goose subprocess adapter
description: Manage Goose process lifecycle: construct CLI args, set environment variables, launch subprocess, capture output, handle exit codes (FR-005, FR-008).
design: In pkg/cli/agent/goose.go, implement GooseAdapter struct with fields: repoRoot string, headless bool, maxTurns int. Methods: NewGooseAdapter(repoRoot string, headless bool, maxTurns int). IsInstalled() uses exec.LookPath('goose') + runs 'goose --version' to verify Block variant. BuildArgs(instructionFile string) returns []string with: 'run', '-i', instructionFile, '--no-session', '--with-builtin', 'developer', '--max-turns', maxTurns. If headless: add '-q'. BuildEnv(resolvedEnvVars map[string]string) merges os.Environ() with Goose-specific vars: GOOSE_PROVIDER, GOOSE_MODEL, GOOSE_PROVIDER__API_KEY, GOOSE_PROVIDER__HOST from config, plus GOOSE_MODE=auto and GOOSE_DISABLE_SESSION_NAMING=true. Execute(instructionFile string, envVars map[string]string, logFile *os.File) creates exec.Command, sets Dir/Env, pipes stdout/stderr to io.MultiWriter(os.Stdout/Stderr, logFile), runs command, returns exit code. WritePID(pidFile string) writes process PID for sl agent stop. InstallInstructions() returns 'brew install block-goose-cli'.
acceptance_criteria: IsInstalled() correctly detects goose presence. BuildArgs produces correct flag set for headless and non-headless modes. Execute captures output to log file while streaming to terminal. Exit code is correctly captured. PID file is written during execution.

title: Implement agent recipe YAML loading
description: Load optional agent-recipe.yaml from spec directory to customize agent execution behavior.
design: In pkg/cli/agent/types.go, add AgentRecipe and RecipeExtension types (already defined in data-model.md). In a new method LoadRecipe(specDir string) (*AgentRecipe, error): check if specDir/agent-recipe.yaml exists. If not, return nil, nil (optional). If exists, parse YAML into AgentRecipe struct. Validate fields (max_turns > 0 if set, extensions have required fields). In context.go Build(): if recipe has instructions, append them to the instruction file under '## Additional Project Instructions' section. In goose.go: if recipe has extensions, add --with-builtin or --with-extension flags. If recipe has settings, merge into env vars. If recipe has max_turns, override the default.
acceptance_criteria: Missing recipe file returns nil without error. Valid recipe parsed correctly. Recipe instructions appear in generated instruction file. Recipe extensions added to Goose CLI args. Recipe max_turns overrides default.

title: Create pkg/cli/agent/ package structure
description: Create the new pkg/cli/agent/ package directory with placeholder files for the agent execution subsystem.
design: Create directory pkg/cli/agent/ with the following empty Go files, each with package declaration and a brief doc comment: types.go (data types), runner.go (orchestrator), context.go (context builder), goose.go (Goose adapter), selector.go (task selection), store.go (run persistence). Each file needs only 'package agent' and a doc comment for now.
acceptance_criteria: Directory pkg/cli/agent/ exists with 6 Go files. Each file compiles (go build ./pkg/cli/agent/...). No functionality yet — just scaffolding.

title: Implement AgentRun JSON persistence store
description: Persist AgentRun metadata as JSON files in .agent-runs/ directory for status queries and history.
design: In pkg/cli/agent/store.go, implement RunStore struct with basePath field. Methods: NewRunStore(specDir string) creates store pointing to specDir/.agent-runs/. Save(run *AgentRun) writes JSON to basePath/<run.ID>.json and copies to basePath/latest.json. Load(runID string) reads specific run. LoadLatest() reads latest.json. EnsureRunDir(runID string) creates basePath/<runID>/ for artifacts. ListRuns() returns all run IDs sorted by date. Use os.MkdirAll for directory creation, json.MarshalIndent for readable output.
acceptance_criteria: RunStore creates .agent-runs/ directory on first save. Save+Load round-trips AgentRun correctly. latest.json always points to most recent run. ListRuns returns IDs in chronological order.

title: Implement Goose environment variable mapping from agent config
description: Map SpecLedger agent config keys to Goose-specific environment variables for subprocess injection (FR-010).
design: In pkg/cli/agent/goose.go, implement BuildGooseEnv(resolvedConfig map[string]string) map[string]string. Mapping: agent.provider -> GOOSE_PROVIDER, agent.model -> GOOSE_MODEL, agent.api-key -> GOOSE_PROVIDER__API_KEY (also set provider-specific key like ANTHROPIC_API_KEY based on provider value), agent.base-url -> GOOSE_PROVIDER__HOST. Always set GOOSE_MODE=auto and GOOSE_DISABLE_SESSION_NAMING=true. Pass through any agent.env.* keys directly. Use existing config.ResolveAgentEnv() from pkg/cli/config/merge.go to get the resolved config map, then transform to Goose-specific naming.
acceptance_criteria: Mapping produces correct Goose env vars for anthropic provider. Provider-specific API key is set (e.g., ANTHROPIC_API_KEY). GOOSE_MODE=auto always present. Custom agent.env vars pass through unchanged.

title: Implement headless execution mode
description: Support non-interactive execution for CI/CD via --headless flag: auto-approve tools, suppress prompts, quiet output (FR-009).
design: In pkg/cli/agent/runner.go and goose.go: When headless=true, (1) GooseAdapter sets GOOSE_MODE=auto in env, adds -q flag, (2) Runner skips stale task interactive prompt (auto-skip stale tasks), (3) Runner suppresses all interactive prompts. (4) Ensure auth can work with env var SPECLEDGER_AUTH_TOKEN as alternative to stored credentials. In pkg/cli/commands/agent.go: document --headless flag behavior. Ensure all output goes to stdout/stderr (no TUI components) when headless.
acceptance_criteria: sl agent run --headless executes without any interactive prompts. GOOSE_MODE=auto is set in subprocess environment. Stale tasks auto-skipped. Output suitable for CI log capture.

title: User Story 1: Local Agent Task Pickup
description: Core value proposition: sl agent run reads tasks, selects ready ones, constructs context, launches Goose, captures results, updates status. Priority P1.

title: Validate quickstart.md workflow end-to-end
description: Verify the quickstart.md guide works from start to finish and update any discrepancies.
design: Follow each step in specledger/599-agent-task-execution/quickstart.md manually. Verify: sl doctor output, goose --version, sl auth status, sl issue list, sl agent run --dry-run, sl agent status. Fix any command syntax mismatches, incorrect flag names, or missing prerequisites in the quickstart. Ensure all referenced commands exist and produce expected output.
acceptance_criteria: Every command in quickstart.md works as documented. No broken or missing commands. Output matches what quickstart promises.

title: Implement task status updates during execution
description: Update issue status in JSONL store as execution progresses: open -> in_progress -> closed/needs_review (FR-006, FR-012).
design: In pkg/cli/agent/runner.go, add methods: claimTask(taskID string) updates status to in_progress via issueStore.Update(). completeTask(taskID string, result TaskResult) checks DoD items — if all DoD items are mechanical (no manual verification), set status=closed and ClosedAt=now; if any DoD item requires manual verification, set status=needs_review. failTask(taskID string, errorMsg string) keeps status=in_progress and appends error to Notes field. Use existing pkg/issues store.Update() with IssueUpdate{Status, Notes, ClosedAt}. For DoD verification: parse DefinitionOfDone.Items, check if any item text contains keywords like 'manual', 'verify', 'review', 'confirm' — those require needs_review status.
acceptance_criteria: Task transitions open->in_progress on claim. Successful tasks with no manual DoD items transition to closed. Tasks with manual DoD items transition to needs_review. Failed tasks stay in_progress with error in Notes. File locking prevents concurrent modification.

title: Setup Phase
description: Project initialization: create pkg/cli/agent/ package, register sl agent command group, add Goose to DefaultAgents, add .agent-runs/ to gitignore.

title: Implement cycle detection and stale task handling
description: Handle edge cases: circular dependencies, stale in_progress tasks from crashed runs, all-tasks-complete scenario.
design: In pkg/cli/agent/selector.go: DetectStale() finds in_progress tasks and returns them. In runner.go Run(): before task discovery, call DetectStale(). If stale tasks found and not headless, prompt user to retry or skip (print list and ask). If headless, log warning and skip stale tasks. Call store.DetectCycles() and if cycles found, log the cycle IDs as errors and mark affected tasks as skipped in run results. After all tasks complete, print summary including completed/failed/skipped counts. If all tasks were already closed (nothing to do), exit with code 2.
acceptance_criteria: Circular dependencies detected and reported as errors. Stale tasks from previous runs detected and handled. All-complete scenario exits cleanly with code 2. Skipped tasks recorded in run metadata.

title: Implement sl agent logs command
description: View captured output from a specific task execution, with tail and follow modes (FR-007).
design: In pkg/cli/commands/agent.go, add VarAgentLogsCmd. Args: task-id (required positional). Flags: --spec, --run (default latest), --tail N, --follow. RunE: (1) Detect spec, load run, (2) Find TaskResult matching task-id, (3) Read output log file at run-dir/task-<id>-output.log, (4) If --tail, show last N lines, (5) If --follow and run is active, use fsnotify or poll to tail the file, (6) Print raw log content. Register as subcommand of VarAgentCmd.
acceptance_criteria: sl agent logs <id> displays full log output. --tail 20 shows last 20 lines. Missing log file produces clear error. Invalid task ID produces clear error.

title: Polish and Cross-Cutting Concerns
description: Documentation, error messages, edge case handling, code cleanup, quickstart validation.

title: Add .agent-runs/ to project gitignore
description: Ensure agent execution artifacts are not tracked in git.
design: Add '.agent-runs/' entry to the project's .gitignore file (or specledger/.gitignore if that's the convention). These are ephemeral, machine-specific runtime artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: .agent-runs/ directories are gitignored. 'git status' does not show .agent-runs/ contents after creating a test directory.

title: Implement core runner orchestrator
description: The main orchestration loop: detect spec, verify prerequisites, discover tasks, execute sequentially, update status, save run results. This is the heart of sl agent run (FR-002, FR-003, FR-005, FR-006).
design: In pkg/cli/agent/runner.go, implement Runner struct with fields: selector *TaskSelector, contextBuilder *ContextBuilder, goose *GooseAdapter, runStore *RunStore, issueStore *issues.Store. Methods: NewRunner(specDir, repoRoot, branch string, headless bool, maxTurns int) wires all dependencies. Run(taskID string) is the main loop: (1) Call selector.DetectStale() and warn about stale tasks, (2) If taskID non-empty call selector.SelectByID() else selector.SelectReady(), (3) Create AgentRun via NewAgentRun(), save initial state, (4) For each task: update issue status to in_progress, build context via contextBuilder.Build(), create log file, execute via goose.Execute(), capture exit code, build TaskResult, update issue status (closed/needs_review/keep in_progress), append result to run, save run state, (5) Set run status to completed/failed, save final state. PreflightChecks() verifies: goose installed, clean git state (git status --porcelain), spec dir exists with issues.jsonl, current branch matches spec context.
acceptance_criteria: Runner executes tasks in priority order. Status transitions work correctly (open->in_progress->closed). Failed tasks don't block subsequent tasks. Run metadata saved after each task. PreflightChecks catches all error conditions (no goose, dirty git, missing spec).

title: Implement task claim locking for concurrent safety
description: Prevent concurrent execution of the same task when multiple agents run in parallel (FR-012).
design: In pkg/cli/agent/runner.go, use the existing gofrs/flock file locking from pkg/issues/store.go for task claim. Additionally, write agent.pid file to .agent-runs/ on start, check for existing PID file before starting (if PID file exists and process is running, abort with 'agent already running for this spec'). Use os.FindProcess + signal 0 to check if PID is alive. Clean up PID file on exit (normal or signal). This prevents two sl agent run instances on the same spec.
acceptance_criteria: Second sl agent run for same spec fails with 'agent already running' error. PID file cleaned up on normal exit. Stale PID file (dead process) detected and overwritten. File lock on issues.jsonl prevents status update races.

title: Implement task selector with priority ordering
description: Select ready-to-execute tasks from the issue store, respecting dependencies and priority ordering (FR-002, FR-003).
design: In pkg/cli/agent/selector.go, implement TaskSelector struct wrapping a *issues.Store. Methods: SelectReady() calls store.ListReady(nil), sorts results by Priority ascending then CreatedAt ascending, returns sorted []*issues.Issue. SelectByID(taskID string) validates the task exists, is open, and is not blocked — returns error if blocked. DetectStale() finds issues with status=in_progress (from previous failed runs) and returns them for retry/skip decision. Use existing pkg/issues/store.go ListReady() and Get() methods.
acceptance_criteria: SelectReady returns tasks in priority order. Blocked tasks are excluded. SelectByID rejects blocked tasks with descriptive error. DetectStale finds orphaned in_progress tasks.

title: Implement sl agent stop command
description: Gracefully stop a running agent by sending SIGTERM, with --force for SIGKILL after timeout (FR-013).
design: In pkg/cli/commands/agent.go, add VarAgentStopCmd. Flags: --spec, --force. RunE: (1) Detect spec, (2) Read PID from .agent-runs/agent.pid, (3) If no PID file, print 'No agent running' and exit, (4) Send syscall.SIGTERM to PID, (5) Wait up to 30s for process to exit (poll with os.FindProcess + signal 0), (6) If --force or timeout, send syscall.SIGKILL, (7) Update latest run status to 'stopped' if applicable, (8) Clean up PID file. Register as subcommand of VarAgentCmd. Note: SIGTERM/SIGKILL are Unix-only — document this limitation.
acceptance_criteria: sl agent stop sends SIGTERM to running agent. Agent has 30s to complete current turn. --force sends SIGKILL immediately. PID file cleaned up. Run status updated to stopped. No-agent-running case handled gracefully.

title: Implement AgentRun and TaskResult types
description: Define the core data types for tracking agent execution: AgentRun (a single invocation of sl agent run) and TaskResult (outcome of executing one task).
design: In pkg/cli/agent/types.go, implement: RunStatus type (running/completed/failed/stopped), TaskResultStatus type (success/failed/skipped/needs_review), AgentRun struct with fields (ID, SpecContext, Status, StartedAt, EndedAt, TasksTotal/Completed/Failed/Skipped, TaskResults, AgentCommand, AgentModel, AgentProvider, Headless, Branch, Error), TaskResult struct with fields (TaskID, TaskTitle, Status, StartedAt, EndedAt, ExitCode, OutputLog, InstructionFile, Commits, Error, DoDVerified). Add constructor NewAgentRun(specContext, branch string, headless bool) that generates run ID format 'run-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS-<4hex>'. See data-model.md for complete field reference.
acceptance_criteria: Types compile. NewAgentRun generates valid IDs. JSON marshal/unmarshal round-trips correctly. All fields from data-model.md are represented.
